notes: Largest UK port by tonnage 1967. London Docks closed 1967 onwards; East London lost ~150,000 jobs 1966–1976 (~20% of area jobs, LDDC). Port reopened 1972 as container terminal at Tilbury.
Second-largest stevedore base. Retrofitted for containers early 1970s. Employment rate collapsed faster than non-port districts 1971–1991.
High tonnage, low dock-labour intensity — already modernised by 1967.
Oil/bulk, not general cargo. Dock-labour light.
Developed as alternative to London during 1968–1970 Tilbury ban; became UK's largest container port within a few years.
Major ports = 95% of 1967 foreign tonnage; ~80 minor commercial ports existed additionally (ports.org.uk).
